summary.uniqueTrackCountBasis: Computed from all Spotify playlist rows in this snapshot.

analysis.headline: K-pop becomes the center, then splits into softer, brighter, electronic, and dance lanes.
points: Early versions center on K-pop bright pop, K-pop electronic, Classical; latest versions center on K-pop bright pop, K-pop electronic, K-pop dance.
The big shift is v1 to v2: classical and score fall away, and K-pop becomes the stable core.
After v2, the movement is mostly inside K-pop: bright pop, electronic pop, soft/R&B, dance, and vocal/solo trade places.
analysis.sourceBoundary: Dates use local Spotify account-cache estimates where recovered; unresolved dates stay marked instead of guessed.

playCounts.playCountDefinition: Every nonzero track listening event in the Spotify export.
playCounts.streams30sDefinition: Listening events with at least 30 seconds played are also retained as streams30s.
playCounts.versionPlayCountDefinition: Every nonzero track listening event after the song was added to that playlist version and before the next Driving version date. Spotify export does not include playlist-source context.
